compiled_state.title: As a performance engineer, I can compare new ticket CLI latency against a reliable pre-refactor baseline
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Capture per-op hyperfine baselines for the ticket CLI's hot paths on the pre-refactor codebase ŌĆö before Story A lands any bash-native changes. Commit the baselines as a test fixture so Story G's regression suite can compare against a known-good reference.

**Why**: SC6 (>10% regression gate) and SC7 (Ōēź60% aggregate reduction) require a pre-refactor baseline. Story G is the last story in the chain ŌĆö by the time it runs, the Python-backed `_ensure_initialized` and `write_commit_event` are gone and the baseline is irrecoverable without a git-checkout dance. Capturing the baseline at the head of the chain is the only way to make those SCs verifiable.

**Scope**:
- IN: Per-op hyperfine measurements (10 warm runs each) for `ticket show`, `ticket list`, `ticket create`, `ticket link`, `ticket transition`, `ticket comment`, `ticket tag`, `ticket untag`, `ticket edit` on the current codebase (Linux, reference hardware); a committed baseline fixture at `tests/fixtures/ticket-cli-baseline.json` capturing per-op mean, p50, p95, stddev; a benchmark runner script that produces the same JSON shape and is reusable by Story G.
- OUT: Running the benchmark in CI; cross-platform baselines (Story F addresses platform variance); any bash-native implementation work.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `tests/fixtures/ticket-cli-baseline.json` exists and contains per-op mean/p50/p95/stddev for the 9 hot ops measured on the current pre-refactor codebase
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: "A new regression test suite measures per-call latency for each covered operation and fails if SC1/SC2 regress by >10%"
- When this story is complete, a reusable benchmark runner script produces the same JSON shape and is invokable by Story G's regression suite
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: "A representative /dso:sprint workflow trace ... demonstrates Ōēź60% aggregate ticket-CLI wall-clock reduction compared to the current dispatcher, measured end-to-end"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic (benchmark runner script)

## Considerations

- [Testing] Reference hardware must be documented alongside the baseline so Story G can detect environment drift

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue ŌĆö one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
